title: Pronunciation specialist guide (foreign-language / names / archaic terms)
context: The narration & pronunciation style guide (style-001) defines what to flag for pronunciation review, but a specialist reviewer needs detailed guidance on how to evaluate and transcribe foreign-language passages, proper names from non-English traditions, and archaic terms. A mispronounced word actively miseducates a literacy learner. The specialist guide extends style-001 for the pronunciation reviewer role, which requires language skill beyond the general QA pool. Depends on style-001.
objective: Write and publish a specialist guide for pronunciation reviewers covering evaluation of foreign-language passages, non-English proper names, and archaic terms, with IPA transcription conventions and worked examples.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the credential bar and skill requirements for a pronunciation reviewer per language type (e.g. native speaker, linguist, or documented proficiency).
Provides guidance for foreign-language passages: how to source authoritative IPA transcriptions, how to evaluate a narrator's rendering, and how to document corrections in the pronunciation lexicon.

Provides guidance for non-English proper names (place names, personal names, historical figures): authoritative reference sources per language family and how to handle anglicized vs. native-language pronunciations.

Provides guidance for archaic English and technical terms: reference sources, IPA transcription conventions, and when anglicization is acceptable vs. when the period or original pronunciation is preferred for a literacy context.
Includes ≥3 worked examples covering at least one foreign-language passage, one non-English name, and one archaic term.
Is published, versioned, and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
resources: read-aloud-audio-style-001 (narration & pronunciation style guide, lexicon conventions)
read-aloud-audio-doc-001 (reviewer workflow + QA rubric, pronunciation axis)
PLAN.md (medium risk tier for pronunciation-critical content)
Forvo and other community pronunciation references
output: A versioned pronunciation specialist guide document (docs/pronunciation-specialist-guide.md), CC-BY-4.0, covering reviewer skill requirements, foreign-language evaluation, proper-name handling, archaic-term conventions, IPA transcription guidance, and worked examples.
